summary: Raspberries, though untested directly, are inferred to have anti-inflammatory and blood sugar-regulating effects due to shared characteristics—such as high polyphenol content—with other berries known to benefit individuals with type 2 diabetes. This makes them consistent with current recommendations for reducing both postprandial hyperglucemia and systemic inflammation via plant-based nutrition.
